framing: These thresholds are PILOT-CALIBRATED DIAGNOSTIC PARAMETERS, NOT hypothesis-test cutoffs. Per formalization_v1.2 §9, paper claims rest on continuous effect sizes + bootstrap CIs; binary classifications under these thresholds are diagnostic-only and reported with sensitivity sweeps in appendix.

M2.definition: median of \widetilde{M_2} = |J*(d)| - |loadbearing(d)| across the 50 v1.0 pilot examples (M2 is condition-independent — same J*(d) and load-bearing dims regardless of executor)
M2.value_pending_reason: v1.0 pilot data was deleted in 2026-05-04 cleanup (task #9). Re-derive from current INDEX.jsonl + spec_dataset r* annotations once pass-2 v1.3 completes; or fall back to v1.3 holdout 10-example subset (mark calibration_source as v1_3_holdout_10).

M3.definition: median of \widetilde{M_3} = |declared △ produced| / |J*(d)∩R1| across the v1.3 self-claim conditions on a 10-example held-out subset (excluded from §V headline reporting)
M3.value_pending_reason: Computed after v1.3 holdout-10 executor + judge runs complete; recorded here at that time before §V analysis.

M4.definition: Classify as 'RX boosted' if \widetilde{M_4} > 0 (i.e., projection_driven scores higher on RX dims than direct_naive on same example).
M4.rationale: Sign-only threshold; no sensitivity sweep needed.

c_q_sweep.definition: Continuous-cutoff parameter for declared dims in M3 symmetric difference (q_ij > c_q means 'declared').

c_v_sweep.definition: Continuous-cutoff parameter for produced dims in M3 symmetric difference (v_ij > c_v means 'produced').

deviation_protocol: After v1.3 rerun completes and ε_M2 + ε_M3 are filled in (replacing nulls), this file is hashed (SHA-256) and timestamped before §V headline analysis runs. Post-hoc threshold changes require a separate `_diagnostic_thresholds_v2.json` file and are reported as sensitivity check, not as the published threshold.
scripts_consuming_this_file: scripts/exp2_aggregate.py — does NOT consume thresholds (continuous metric primary)
